thread.title: Karnataka Bypolls 2024: Gloom elsewhere, but in Karnataka, triple joy for Congress
thread.title_full: Karnataka Bypolls 2024: Gloom elsewhere, but in Karnataka, triple joy for Congress

author: Sridhar Vivan
title: Karnataka Bypolls 2024: Gloom elsewhere, but in Karnataka, triple joy for Congress
text:
Karnataka Bypolls 2024: Gloom elsewhere, but in Karnataka, triple joy for Congress
Congress workers celebrate after the party’s win, in Bengaluru on Saturday
Party’s clean sweep in 3 constituencies’ bypolls underscores its strength in state
The political dynamics following the recent by-elections reflect a tale of three states: while Maharashtra’s resounding win bolstered the BJP-led NDA, and Jharkhand’s verdict served as a consolidation point for the INDIA alliance; Karnataka emerged as a significant morale booster for the ruling Congress. The party’s clean sweep in the three constituencies of Channapatna, Shiggaon, and Sandur underscored its growing strength in the state as it defeated both the combined strength of BJP and JDS, with its tally now rising to 138 seats in the assembly.
In Karnataka, Congress not only retained Sandur—vacated by E Tukaram after his Lok Sabha victory in Ballari—but also wrested Channapatna and Shiggaon, traditionally NDA strongholds, adding two crucial seats to its count. This reversal comes with its own ironies. In Maharashtra, the BJP and its allies struggled in the Lok Sabha polls but made gains in assembly elections. Karnataka (except in Sandur), in contrast, saw the Congress falter in the Lok Sabha elections in Channapatna (part of Bengaluru Rural) and Shiggaon (part of Haveri parliamentary constituency) but managed a strong comeback in the assembly bypolls.
Congress credits leadership
Deputy Chief Minister DK Shivakumar quickly credited the bypoll victory to Chief Minister Siddaramaiah’s leadership and the government’s flagship “five guarantees,” which have struck a chord with the electorate. Speaking on Saturday, Shivakumar described the results as a “prelude to the 2028 assembly elections,” expressing confidence in the party’s ability to secure another term in the state. “The by-elections, held on November 13 with results announced today, reflect a deepening of Congress’s hold in Karnataka amid shifting electoral patterns. The party’s success could serve as a significant counter-narrative to the BJP’s dominance at the national level and boost its confidence as it positions itself within the broader INDIA alliance post 2024 general elections,” he said.
National perspective
A political pundit said, “The contrasting outcomes in Maharashtra and Karnataka highlight the nuanced electoral strategies at play. Maharashtra’s BJP and allies turned their assembly poll performance into a rebound after Lok Sabha setbacks. However, Congress’s Karnataka strategy—a blend of strong grassroots leadership and welfare-driven promises—allowed it to defy its earlier Lok Sabha underperformance and consolidate power at the state level. As Congress celebrates this victory, it will likely analyse the Karnataka model to replicate its success in other states while addressing weaknesses exposed in the Lok Sabha polls.”
3 BIG WINNERS
1 Siddaramaiah
This by-election victory marks a personal win for Chief Minister Siddaramaiah, who has been under scrutiny due to probes into the MUDA, Waqf, and ST Development Corporation cases. After Congress’ defeat in the Lok Sabha elections, ministers had called for a reassessment of the Guarantee schemes. However, this victory reinforces the legitimacy of the party’s guarantee schemes and strengthens Siddaramaiah’s leadership, positioning him better for the upcoming Zilla Panchayat, Taluk Panchayat, and BBMP elections.
2 DK Shivakumar
The Deputy Chief Minister has scored a significant personal win with this victory. He faced a setback when his younger brother, DK Suresh, lost the Bengaluru Rural parliamentary constituency. Ironically, CP Yogeeshwara, who had campaigned for the BJP and played a key role in the defeat of Suresh, became a valuable asset for DK Shivakumar. Demonstrating political savvy, DKS brought the disgruntled CPY into the Congress fold and awarded him a party ticket, following the principle of “my enemy’s enemy is my friend.” This helped secure a Congress victory in the Vokkaliga heartland.
3 CP Yogeeshwara
Following his victory, the seasoned turncoat politician likened himself to an IPL player, stating that he can belong to any party but will always ensure his team clinches the win. Notably, Yogeeshwara, who had never secured over 1 lakh votes since his first contest in 1999, reached this milestone for the first time.
3 BIG LOSERS
1 HD Kumaraswamy
Union Minister HD Kumaraswamy regards Ramanagar district as the place that gave him political rebirth. Although he hails from Hassan district, it was in Ramanagar where he re-established his leadership, winning from the constituency during his two terms as Chief Minister. He secured victories in 2004, 2008, and 2013. In 2018, he contested both the Ramanagar and Channapatna constituencies, winning both, but later vacated Ramanagar to ensure his wife, Anitha Kumaraswamy, retained the seat. He won Channapatna in both 2018 and 2023. After securing a win in Mandya Lok Sabha polls, Kumaraswamy relinquished Channapatna to make way for his son Nikhil. However, in this personal battle against the DKS family, he lost.
2 Nikhil Kumaraswamy
Nikhil Kumaraswamy faced a disastrous defeat in the Channapatna by-election, marking a hat-trick of losses. He had previously lost the Mandya parliamentary constituency in 2019 and Ramanagar in 2023, and now Channapatna in 2024. As the third-generation leader from the prominent HD Deve Gowda political family, Nikhil is considered the JDS scion, but this string of defeats will raise questions about his political future for now.
3 BY Vijayendra
This by-election served as a critical test for BJP State unit president BY Vijayendra. While exit polls had predicted two seats for NDA and one for Congress, the results now put Vijayendra under intense pressure due to the party’s poor performance, particularly when his leadership was being questioned by rebels in the party. Disgruntled BJP leader Basanagouda Patil Yatnal did not mince words, calling for Vijayendra’s resignation. Additionally, CP Yogeeshwara alleged that Vijayendra had conspired with JD(S) to deny him a ticket.
The political dynamics following the recent by-elections reflect a tale of three states: while Maharashtra’s resounding win bolstered the BJP-led NDA, and Jharkhand’s verdict served as a consolidation point for the INDIA alliance; Karnataka emerged as a significant morale booster for the ruling Congress. GALLERIES
